title: Safe-settings suborg-level configuration
description: Schema for .github/suborgs/*.yml — suborg-level safe-settings configuration. Defines which repos belong to the suborg and what settings to apply.

suborgrepos.description: Glob patterns matching repository names. Repos whose names match any pattern are included in this suborg.

suborgteams.description: Team slugs. Repos that belong to any of these teams are included in this suborg.

suborgproperties.description: Custom property filters. Repos with matching custom property values are included in this suborg.

property_name.description: The name of the custom property

collaborators.description: Collaborators: give specific users access to any repository.

milestones.description: Milestones: define milestones for Issues and Pull Requests

RepositorySettings.description: Repository settings. Use force_create to create the repository if it does not exist, and template to specify a template repository to use when creating it.

auto_init.description: Create an initial commit with empty README. Keep this set to true in most cases since many of the policies below cannot be implemented on bare repos

gitignore_template.description: Desired language or platform [.gitignore template](https://github.com/github/gitignore) to apply. Use the name of the template without the extension. For example, 'Haskell'.

license_template.description: Choose an [open source license template](https://choosealicense.com/) that best suits your needs, and then use the [license keyword](https://help.github.com/articles/licensing-a-repository/#searching-github-by-license-type) as the `license_template` string. For example, 'mit' or 'mpl-2.0'.

topics.description: A list of topics to set on the repository

security.description: Settings for Code security and analysis

force_create.description: If true, create the repository if it does not already exist.

template.description: Name of a template repository to use when creating a new repository.

LabelSettings.description: Labels: define labels for Issues and Pull Requests

color.description: The hexadecimal color code for the label. If including a `#`, make sure to wrap it with quotes!

oldname.description: Include the old name to rename an existing label

exclude.description: Ignore any labels matching these regexes (don't delete them)

CollaboratorSettings.description: A collaborator entry giving a specific user access to a repository.

exclude.description: You can exclude a list of repos for this collaborator and all repos except these repos would have this collaborator

include.description: You can include a list of repos for this collaborator and only those repos would have this collaborator

state.description: The state of the milestone. Either `open` or `closed`

name.description: If the name of the branch value is specified as `default`, then the app will create a branch protection rule to apply against the default branch in the repo
The name of the deployment environment

wait_timer.description: The amount of time to delay a job after the job is initially triggered (in minutes)

reviewers.description: The people or teams that may review jobs that reference the environment

id.description: The id of the user or team who can review the deployment

deployment_branch_policy.description: The type of deployment branch policy for this environment

protected_branches.description: Whether only protected branches can be deployed to this environment

custom_branch_policies.description: Whether only branches that match the specified name patterns can deploy to this environment

prevent_self_review.description: Whether or not a user who created the job is prevented from approving their own job

visibility.description: The visibility of the variable. Can be `all`, `private`, or `selected`
